The stored procedure cleans inactive users and logs changes.  
It begins by creating a transaction to ensure data safety.  
Next the system deletes rows where status is Inactive.  
Before deletion the user ID and old status are saved.  
A new row is added to the status log table.  
The modification date is set to the current timestamp.  
Error handling is included to catch any runtime issues.  
If an error occurs the transaction is rolled back immediately.  
The procedure returns a message describing any failure found.  
You may call this routine whenever cleanup is required.  
The code ensures consistency across both tables involved.  
Now we check the total count of removed records.  
The process ends with a commit if no errors arise.  
A palindrome appears here: level.
